name: nephthys (sister
shortDescription: Nephthys is an ancient Egyptian goddess associated with mourning, night, rivers, and protection. She is primarily known as the sister of Isis and the wife of Set.
description: Nephthys, also known as Nebthet, is a goddess in ancient Egyptian religion. Her name translates to "Lady of the House" or "Mistress of the House," although the exact meaning is debated. She is primarily known as the sister of Isis, the wife of Set, and the mother of Anubis (in some traditions). Nephthys is associated with mourning, lamentation, the night, rivers, and protection. While often depicted as a protective deity, particularly in funerary contexts, she also embodies the liminal spaces between life and death. In mythology, Nephthys plays a vital role in the Osiris myth. She is known for her compassion towards Isis and her support in the resurrection of Osiris after his murder by Set. Despite being Set's wife, she aided Isis in her quest to restore Osiris, demonstrating her loyalty and empathy. Nephthys' role in funerary rituals and her association with the afterlife made her a significant figure in ancient Egyptian beliefs about death and rebirth. She is often depicted on sarcophagi and in tombs, offering protection to the deceased. Her image frequently appears alongside Isis, emphasizing their sisterly bond and their shared role in safeguarding the dead. Representations of Nephthys often show her with the hieroglyphs for her name on her head, which usually include a basket on top of a house.

sources.title: Myths and Symbols of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Lucia Gahlin

sources.title: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
